headline: Marine Le Pen conviction: 'It's very French to think that preventing one person from running undermines democracy as a whole'
description: In the far-right leader's appeals trial, judges confirmed, once again, the courts' wariness of intervening too heavily in the political arena.

headline: British Museum director on Bayeux Tapestry loan: 'A gesture of confidence, friendship and, above all, trust'

'Everything here will burn': Raging wildfire wreaks havoc in France's Drôme mountains
description: Hundreds of firefighters and military personnel have faced a vast wildfire sweeping through more than 3,500 hectares of this rugged region north of Provence – leading three villages, two campsites and 450 children at summer camp to be evacuated.

headline: Strait of Hormuz deadlock could lead Trump into sporadic war with Iran
description: The renewed US and Iranian strikes on Wednesday, despite the memorandum of understanding reached between the two countries, jeopardize hopes for a swift end to the crisis unpopular with the American public.

headline: France's powerful attack secures World Cup semi-final spot
description: Kylian Mbappé and his teammates dominated the Atlas Lions (2-0), confirming their offensive potential. They will play the winner of Spain vs. Belgium on July 14 in Dallas.

headline: Children at the office during the heatwave: 'They have a meeting room with coloring books'
description: M Le magazine du Monde columnist Guillemette Faure examines how the heatwave in France has brought employees' children into their parents' workplaces.

How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
description: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.

headline: After a year eyeing France's presidency, Bardella has to settle for deputy
description: On Marine Le Pen's first campaign trip after being cleared to run for president despite her conviction, her would-be stand-in Jordan Bardella looked stern at her side.

headline: Renewed US-Iran hostilities highlight Tehran's deep mistrust of negotiations
description: The exchanges of strikes in recent hours have bolstered the arguments of the ultraconservative wing of Iran's theocratic regime, which regards talks with Washington as criminal. The cease-fire is hanging by a thread.
